calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-25-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Bruce Feder for and with Stuart Lowe and for Adrienne Lowe (Successor Co-Trustees).

The Court noted that there is no appearance from Curtis Lowe. Mr. Feder stated that he has had no communication with Curtis in three weeks, that Curtis has voiced no objections or intent to oppose, and that Curtis' issue is with distribution. Mr. Feder also stated that Curtis has not opposed sanctions or the surcharge, that there are assets outside the Trust, that they are asking to be allowed to work on those and that he believes final distribution will occur fairly soon. In addition, Mr. Feder stated that the Siblings have talked about distribution and that they would like him to prepare something formal to present to Curtis. The First Trust Account and Report of Successor Co-Trustees, etc. is taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y. S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: J. Reyes. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES

Off Calendar Mar-26-2018 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Co-Trustees' Accounting And Proposed Form Of Distribution
Off Calendar Feb-26-2018 Continued To Mar-26-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Feder for (Successor Trustees) Stuart Lowe & Adrienne Lowe.

Mr. Feder reported that the accounting has been completed, that it is being reviewed by his clients and that they expect to be able to file it soon. The status of filing an accounting was continued to March 26, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Jung. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Co-Trustees' Accounting And Proposed Form Of Distribution
Off Calendar Jan-22-2018 Continued To Feb-26-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Bruce Feder for Adrienne Lowe and Stewart Lowe (Trustee).

Mr. Feder requested a continuance stating that the accounting is just about completed and that it is roughly a two and a half year period. Status on filing of the accounting is continued to February 26, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

Petition To Allow First Forensic Accounting Of Successor Co-Trustees For The Period September 2, 2014 To February 27, 2015, And For Approval Of Payment To Forensic Accountant For Services Rendered
Off Calendar Oct-24-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Bruce Feder for and with Adrienne Lowe.
Curtis Lowe in Propria Persona.

Mr. Feder stated that he believes they can settle out the issue of the forensic accounting and issues that occurred when the Successor Trustee took over, if not, they can file a petition. Mr. Feder also asked the Court to grant this as it's the best they can do, spoke of in and out participation with Curtis, stated that Curtis did not provide information and that he did not assist in filling out the schedules. Mr. Lowe stated that he knows he could plug in some gaps, that this is a busy time of year for him and that he has no time. The Petition to Allow First Forensic Accounting of Successor Co-Trustees, etc. is taken under submission. Status on filing of the Co-Trustees' accounting and proposed form of distribution is set for January 22, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Feder requested that Curtis provide information regarding the equity line of credit.

Judge: Andrew Y. S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

Petition To Allow First Forensic Accounting Of Successor Co-Trustees For The Period September 2, 2014 To February 27, 2015, And For Approval Of Payment To Forensic Accountant For Services Rendered

Petition To Allow First Forensic Accounting Of Successor Co-Trustees For The Period September 2, 2014 To February 27, 2015, And For Approval Of Payment To Forensic Accountant For Services Rendered
Off Calendar Aug-23-2017 Continued To Sep-25-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Bruce Feder for and with Adrienne Lowe and for Stewart Lowe (Co-Trustees).
Curtis Lowe (Former Trustee/Beneficiary) in Propria Persona.

The Court noted that the declaration setting forth the amount of sanctions against Curtis Lowe was filed on August 15, 2017 and that 30-day notice was not provided. Mr. Lowe stated that he is going to file objection as to the amount of sanctions. The Petition to Allow First Forensic Accounting of Successor Co-Trustees, etc. is continued to September 25, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Lowe is to file his objection by September 18, 2017.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Aug-15-2016 Continued To Oct-17-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Feder for trust beneficiaries/objectors Adrienne Lowe and Stuart Lowe; Curtis Lowe, petitioner in pro per.

Mr. Feder stated that he had a chat with Mr. Lowe and had encouraged him to work cooperatively with his siblings. He has made arrangements with Martin Harris to perform a forensic accounting, and Mr. Lowe has consented to the use of Mr. Harris. Mr. Lowe stated that he would continue to work cooperatively with his siblings. The status hearing was continued to October 17, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Chan. (Not reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Aug-01-2016 Continued To Aug-15-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Jun-27-2016 Continued To Aug-01-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Feder for Adrienne Lowe & Stuart Lowe. Curtis Lowe, in Propria Persona.

The Court asked if there has been progress. Mr. Feder responded that there has not, that on 6/6/16 his client received a thumb drive but not all of the information was there, that it is not laid out on the excel spreadsheet that had been started, that he received an email from Mr. Lowe stating that he was having difficulty finding help for the accounting, that he would be putting together additional information and that Mr. Feder's clients are tired of him having to appear in court. Mr. Lowe stated that he does not have additional information other than what has been provided, that his work fluctuates and that he has to work when it is available, that he does not have the financial ability to pay sanctions and requested that he be able to meet with his siblings to go over this. Mr. Feder stated that he does not need a court order or Mr. Feder to sit down with his siblings and that nothing happens in between court dates. The Court stated that there has been a good faith effort to work things out, that there has been too many appearances, too many chances and that it will order a forensic accounting. Mr. Feder is to submit a proposed order. The Accounting Petition was continued to August 1, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on the forensic accounting.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Boncato. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Jun-06-2016 Continued To Jun-27-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Curtis Lowe, petitioner in pro per; Bruce Feder for beneficiaries Adrienne Lowe and Stuart Lowe

Mr. Feder advised the court that he parties have been working cooperatively and have made some progress to date. Mr. Lowe had provided him some accounting information last Wednesday, and had provided some additional information to Adrienne Lowe on a flash drive on Thursday. He stated that Ms. Lowe is now going over that information. He stated that his clients are willing to continue working cooperatively with the petitioner, but that Mr. Lowe needs the services of a bookkeeper or accountant to assist him with the completion of the accounting. Mr. Lowe stated his desire to work with his siblings, but that he needs their feedback regarding the information he has provided. Mr. Feder stated that he would try to help Mr. Lowe find an accountant or bookkeeper to assist him. The matter was continued to June 27, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court ordered imposition of sanctions against petitioner in the amount of $25 per day unti the filing of the accounting, stayed until June 27, 2016, with the sanctions to accrue from June 6, 2016 in the event the accounting is not filed by June 27, 2016.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not reported

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar May-16-2016 Continued To Jun-06-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Curtis Lowe in Propria Persona. Bruce Feder for (trustees) Adrienne Lowe and Stuart Lowe.

Mr. Feder reported that the meeting was productive, that it occurred within the time frame, that he spoke with Mr. Lowe this morning and he understands what needs to be done next, that Mr. Lowe had not received the email with the next steps but is aware now, that they need a 3 week continuance, that they will need to amend the accounting, that Mr. Lowe will need to obtain a new person to help him and that Mr. Lowe is to go through his records for the last filed 1040 and other information to help with taxes. Mr. Lowe stated that he needs to get another person to help him with the accounting. The Court stated that it does not want a breakdown, that the sanctions request is ongoing and is just a matter of if it is issued and that it would be helpful for a status report to be filed a few days before the next hearing. The accounting petition was continued to June 6, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Nancy Wong. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar May-02-2016 Continued To May-16-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Curtis Lowe in Propria Persona. Bruce Feder for (co-trustees) Adrienne Lowe and Stuart Lowe who is present in court.

The Court stated that it has received Mr. Feder's recent filing and that there has not been compliance with the Court's order. Mr. Feder stated that they had requested an amended accounting, that some additional information has been provided, that the information is not clear, that he spoke with Mr. Lowe prior to this hearing, that it might help to go through the information along with the trustees and to figure out what is missing, that it can be done at Mr. Feder's office and that it may be effective. The Court asked about the possibility of a forensic accounting and assessing the cost. Mr. Feder stated that it would be quite expensive, that the next step is to sit down face to face, that they should try this in the spirit of getting this done, that he renews his request that sanctions be imposed, that Mr. Lowe has to understand that this is his responsibility and that the burden is not on the current trustees. Mr. Lowe stated that in regards to the questions he provided corrective action, that he is willing to work to resolve the issues, that the current trustees have been in this capacity for 1.5 years and that he was only trustee from August to February. The Court asked Mr. Feder for a sanction amount. Mr. Feder responded $25.00 per day, that the point is to bring it to the table and suggested a 7 day period before the sanctions start. The Court asked Mr. Lowe is he knows what is required, that the Court will impose sanctions but with a stay for 7 days and that if there is not full compliance the sanctions will accumulate until paid. Mr. Lowe asked how to define an accounting to his ability. The Court responded that it was stated in the order, asked Mr. Feder for an order regarding the sanctions to be imposed on 5/9/16, the order to meet face to face and that they must get this done. Mr. Feder stated that the order also compels Mr. Lowe to provide other financial information for the current trustees to move forward. The Court addressed Mr. Lowe and stated that the consequences of not getting this done will be expensive. Mr. Lowe stated that he thinks the meeting will resolve the issues and that the information he has provided is enough. The accounting petition was continued to May 16, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Feb-22-2016 Continued To Mar-14-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Jan-11-2016 Continued To Feb-22-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Curtis Lowe in Propria Persona. Bruce Feder for and with (co-trustee) Adrienne Lowe.

The Court addressed Mr. Lowe regarding the notes from 12/15/15. Mr. Lowe stated that he only has the objections, that he did not see the notes and that he is willing to answer questions and clarify accordingly. Mr. Feder stated that Mr. Lowe indicated that he did not see the 12/22 notes, that Mr. Feder could provide a copy to him, that they had let Mr. Lowe know that they had filed objections, that the accounting needs to be cleaned up, that there are financial transactions that took place prior to his appointment, that some accounts are not formally titled in the trust, that income tax returns are delinquent, that they do not have all of the records that he said he would provide and that they need some documents that are outside of his time of trustee. The Court stated that Mr. Lowe was working with Ms. Nightingale but that there is still no accounting on file. Mr. Feder stated that objections were filed in response to the accounting and other issues. Mr. Lowe stated that he has turned over 99% of the information to the successor trustees, that all of the rental fees are now going to them, that they have representation and all of the information, that he knew there would always be a problem, that he has tried to make things as easy as possible for them, that they will never be satisfied and that they need to step up. Mr. Feder stated that they would like the court to instruct Mr. Lowe to communicate, to work through the gaps in the accounting and information, that there is a failure to maintain records and to provide what the law requires, that Mr. Lowe needs to understand that this is a priority, that they would like the court to set time to come back and to apply sanctions if there is no movement, that Mr. Feder is willing to have Mr. Lowe come to his office and that they are trying to get his accounting and all the information so that they can file taxes. Mr. Lowe stated that since the last hearing he has communicated, that he did file the accounting, that the successor trustees filed their objection 3 days before this hearing, that he is willing to work with them and give them the information but that it cannot encompass his whole life. The Court stated that it expects Mr. Lowe to meet with Mr. Feder by January 22, 2016. The accounting petition was continued to February 22, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Lowe is instructed to submit answers to the examiner's notes and to get the ball moving along, that additional consequences of sanctions will be imposed as of February 22, 2016 if the information needed is not provided. The Court pointed out to Mr. Lowe that the proof of service was not on a judicial counsel form and suggested he check with Ms. Nightingale. The Court instructed Mr. Feder to prepare a proposed order based on today's hearing.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Accounting
Off Calendar Oct-13-2015 Continued To Nov-24-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Feder for (petitioners) Adrienne Lowe and Stuart Lowe who is present. Curtis Lowe in Propria Persona.

Mr. Lowe stated that he needs a continuance, that he is still putting the accounting together, that he can turn over the bank statements to Mr. Feder today, that his wife has been ill and that he has needed time with his family. The Court stated that this needs to get done. Mr. Lowe stated that he needs an additional 45 days and that he will complete it sooner if possible. Mr. Feder stated that after the last hearing was continued he provided Mr. Lowe with a list of names of people who could prepare accountings and that he does not think Mr. Lowe can complete it without assistance. The Court asked Mr. Lowe if he has been in touch with anyone who could help. Mr. Lowe stated that he has not, that he has had obligations to his family but that he could contact someone this week. The status on filing an accounting petition was continued to November 24, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with the understanding that today Mr. Lowe is to provide information to Mr. Feder and to immediately contact someone who can provide assistance. The court clerk provided Mr. Lowe with a list of paralegals.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

Petition To Compel Trustee To Account
Off Calendar Sep-04-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Feder for and with Stuart Lowe and for Adrienne Lowe. Curtis Lowe in Propria Persona.

The Court asked Curtis Lowe if he was here to object. Mr. Lowe stated that he was not and that he was here to provide information. The Court asked Mr. Lowe if he was prepared to do an accounting, that if ordered it will need to be properly formatted and suggested that Mr. Lowe may want to hire assistance such as an attorney or a professional who knows how to prepare the accountings. Mr. Lowe indicated that he was prepared to do so. The Court stated that the petitioner is also asking for an accounting up to the current date, asked if there were any problems with that and stated that it is not sure why the date would go further than his resignation date. Mr. Feder stated that they are not sure that assets have been turned over. The Court stated that the turnover of assets is a different issue, that it cannot compel an accounting for a time in which he was not the trustee. Mr. Lowe stated that his reason for resigning is that he did not have the time needed to manage it, that he has less time now and requested 45 days to file the accounting. The Court stated that it will grant the petition to compel an accounting (subject to receipt/review of the order) for the time period that Mr. Lowe was the trustee, suggested that Mr. Lowe obtain legal advice and that Mr. Feder is to prepare a revised proposed order reflecting the courts determination. The Court encouraged the parties to talk to each other. A status of filing an accounting petition was set for October 13, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)
